verdicts.reason: It renders delivery of a written notice to the person together with an authoritative future demand and adverse consequence for noncompliance.

verdicts.missing: It drops the writ or summons and its authoritative legal demand, carrying only movement of an unspecified thing near someone.
verdicts.reason: This is a generic act that leaves an object near a person, which fits many deliveries and not specifically service of process.

verdicts.missing: It drops the compulsory writ-or-summons content, carrying only someone saying words to another person.
verdicts.reason: Delivering and discussing words is too broad to distinguish service of process from ordinary communication.

verdicts.missing: It drops delivery of a writ or summons, carrying only a person seeing something and knowing words.
verdicts.reason: The asserted scene does not express service or delivery and is compatible with unrelated encounters.

verdicts.missing: It drops the writ or summons and its legal-notice function, carrying only placement of an unspecified thing at someone.
verdicts.reason: This gives a generic delivery skeleton without the criterial legal document or summons content.

verdicts.missing: It drops the writ or summons and authoritative notice, carrying only an unspecified thing touching another person.
verdicts.reason: Physical transfer of any object to a person does not identify service of process.

verdicts.missing: It drops the compulsory writ-or-summons content, carrying delivery and speech about an unspecified thing.
verdicts.reason: The candidate describes a generic handover accompanied by speech, not legally effective delivery of process.
